LinkedIn's "Easy Apply" process doesn't auto-populate profile URL

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Chris Loiselle
9mo
  • Report this post

Is it ironic to anyone else that the "easy apply process" that LinkedIn has does not pull in your LinkedIn profile url in to the easy apply process in a pre-filled fashion? Am I missing a profile setting that I should update to have this auto populated? If so, please let me know! It appears the LinkedIn profile URL form field that companies use in their workflows in the easy apply process is not auto populating. As an end user, I'm using either a copy paste function to pull the profile url in from a browser tab or Word document and then going back to the mobile app workflow (or web based flow) during the easy apply steps to add this manually during the process prior to the submission of a job application. #automation #ai #ATS #Resumeparsing

4
Like Comment

To view or add a comment, sign in

You can literally have a conversation with it. Tell it to review thousands of applications. Find hidden candidates, or send emails in your voice. And with the AI Orchestrator… it’s wild. You assign it tasks in plain English and it executes them in real time, right in front of you. Every decision you make teaches it how you hire. Every hire makes it smarter. Over time, it builds a playbook that’s unique to your company. This is what recruiting should feel like.     Most founders think they need a CTO to build AI-powered apps. Wrong. I watched Sarah, a marketing consultant, go from "I have zero coding skills" to launching an AI-driven client analytics platform in 3 weeks. Not 3 months. Not 3 quarters. 3 weeks. Here's what actually happened: Week 1: She mapped out her vision using Bubble.io. No code. Just logic, workflows, and user experience design. Week 2: We integrated AI APIs for data analysis and automated reporting. The app started recognizing patterns in her clients' campaigns that she'd been tracking manually for years. Week 3: Beta launch. 15 paying customers by day 3. THE BREAKTHROUGH: She stopped waiting for the "perfect technical team" and started building with the tools available today. No-code platforms aren't just for prototypes anymore. They're powering real businesses, serving real customers, generating real revenue. Sarah's app now processes data for 200+ marketing agencies. Built entirely without traditional developers. The gap between idea and impact? It's shrinking fast. Big corporations move slow. They debate. They hire. They delay. Small founders with no-code and AI? They build. They launch. They learn. They scale. That's the competitive advantage. Your technical limitations aren't stopping you from building intelligent applications. Your assumptions about what's required are. The question isn't whether you can compete with tech giants. It's whether you're ready to move faster than they can. Drop a comment if you're curious about turning your business idea into a functioning AI-powered app without hiring developers.

    🔑 Built my own Inventory App with Login System + ChatGPT’s help 🤖 🚀 Prajwal Inventory App – Built with Google Apps Script + Google Sheets 💡 I recently created my own Inventory Management Web App using Google Apps Script (.gs) + HTML/JavaScript, directly connected to Google Sheets as a database — with valuable support from ChatGPT in the process. 👉 Key Features of this Google Apps Script Project: 📊 Dashboard View – Real-time Sales Trends, Purchases, Top Customers, Reports 🏪 Inventory Tracking – Auto Reorder Alerts & Stock Updates synced with Google Sheets 👨💼 Suppliers & Customers Management – Centralized platform for all records 💵 Purchases, Sales, Receipts, Payments – Fully integrated workflow 🔐 Login System – Secured access (any user with a valid User ID & Password can log in) ⚡ Backend: .gs files (Google Apps Script logic) ⚡ Frontend: .html files (UI with HTML, JavaScript) ⚡ Database: Google Sheets (live-connected) This Inventory App is live-updating – every change in Google Sheets instantly reflects in the Web App. 👇 Sneak peek of my work: 📸 Dashboard Overview 📸 Inventory Sheet 👉 Such Google Sheets + Apps Script based Inventory Systems can help businesses of all sizes manage Inventory, Sales & Payments seamlessly. ✨ Upcoming upgrades: PDF/Excel export, Multi-user roles, Notifications and more advanced automation. 💬 If you need the source code for this app, just message me 🔗 Follow Prajwal Ingole for more such projects, automation ideas #GoogleAppsScript #GoogleSheets #InventoryManagement #Automation #WebApp #ChatGPT #HTML #Gemini #LowCode #NoCodeDevelopment #DigitalTransformation #BusinessAutomation #DataDriven #Innovation

    How AI is transforming recruitment: from 3-month hiring cycles to 72-hour shortlists 🚀 At our recent @Mindstone community meetup, John Osei-Kwaku gave us a live demo of SkillsMatch, his AI-powered job matching platform that's tackling one of recruitment's biggest challenges. The problem? John shared how a single AI Engineer job posting attracted over 1,200 applicants - creating overwhelming noise for hiring managers and frustration for qualified candidates. His solution? A skills-based matching system that: • Gives candidates personalized job match scores based on their skill profile • Provides hiring managers with AI-analyzed shortlists highlighting strengths and gaps • Maintains the human element with talent partners facilitating introductions What impressed our community most wasn't just the concept, but that John built this entire platform himself despite not having a coding background. He leveraged AI development tools like bolt.new to bring his vision to life - a perfect example of how accessible building with AI has become. This is exactly why we created the Mindstone community: to showcase practical applications of emerging technologies that solve real-world problems and inspire others to build their own solutions. See the full presentation and live demo here: https://lnkd.in/eKsi85y9 #PracticalAI #FutureOfWork #AIRecruitment

  • Jeroen Terstegge
    9mo Edited
    • Report this post

    😡 How not to do user choice. So, LinkedIn has entered the Age of AI. No surprises there. But this is not how you do user choice properly!! ❌️ Apart from the question, whether consent was necessary or that an opt-out was sufficient, LinkedIn uses a single opt-out choice. You're either all in, or all out. ❌️ Oh yes, in the iOS app, the opt-out didn't work either. I would have preferred granular choices. ✅️ I would have opted in to use my primary posts. They are aimed at the general public anyway. ✅️ I would have opted out to use my replies, as they are aimed at the original poster or part of a debate. ✅️ I would have opted out to use my DM's, as they are private. ✅️ I would have opted out to use my account and profile information. Granular choice is a real thing. And companies must pay attention, so as to not miss out of data use opportunities.

  • João Camarate
    9mo
    • Report this post

    OpenAI has just disrupted Google Adwords and Apple App Store for Software in one fell swoop. Startup product distribution has just changed forever I've just wrapped up watching Sam Altman's open keynote on OpenAI Dev Day (its available on youtube, link in the comments) There was a bunch of really amazing demos, but the most impressive - and possibly game changing one - was how you can now publish Apps inside ChatGPT. ChatGPT can now find "Apps" (i.e. SaaS or other Software Tools) and suggest them to you (advertise) in the flow of conversation. Let's say you ask ChatGPT help to build your pitch deck, it will outline the deck and then suggest Canva (as an app) to build your deck. If you choose to accept to use Canva then ChatGPT will proceed to connect you and work with Canva on your behalf to build the deck. What they just did was to: 1) Advertise an app for you (monetization incoming) 2) Make themselves the operating system on which that app runs (more monetization) So Startups tomorrow will be: 1) Adjusting their product vision to better fit OpenAI's ecosystem, like they did in the past with iOS, Windows, MacOS, Android, etc 2) Funnel their Marketing dollars so that ChatGPT "suggest" their app more frequently and to the right audience (use cases) In turn OpenAI will: 1) Invite you in with a "free" setup and close to no-strings-attached 2) Eventually constrain their terms of service to the point where you are now paying them 20%+ of the revenue you generate over their ecosystem (much like Apple/Google) This is a massive shift in how people find, subscribe and use applications. And it will give OpenAI power to build or destroy companies Exciting :)
